prompt:
With the rising popularity of people who dropped out of college and became successful, more and more young people tend to end their education process as soon as they graduate from school or even earlier. However, there are people who think that the trend to get higher education among younger generation is toxic and meaningless, because if you find a job early, you will be successful in life. 

Personally, I strongly disagree with that point of view. First, when a person graduates from school, he/she has extremely limited knowledge in almost every sphere and no experience in adult life. Getting an university degree can broaden your vision on a particular sphere of interest, help you gain knowledge and skills much more quickier than you'd have gained 'em while working in whatever company. Additionally, while a person studies at university, he/she learns how to live on their owns, separated from their parents, and the adaptation period goes much more easier than if a person decided to find a job right after graduating out of school. 

Also, university is a great place to skyrocket your social skills, soft-skills and common sense. That happens because you interact with people of your age with similar goals, hobbies, manner of talking, and that makes the process of the personality development smooth like butter. On the other hand, when you start working somewhere, there will be a little chance that you'll meet people your age or people you simply would like to interact with. In the end, we are still children at 18 y.o. 

In conclusion, I want to say that every single person should decide for themselves whether they need degree or not; whether they need to find a job after school or not. After all, every individual has their own perception on any decision in their life.

messages.content:
You are an expert CEFR writing assessor. You MUST follow the methodology below and output ONLY valid JSON.

Assessment order is mandatory:
level determination -> communicative task achievement -> error markup -> strengths -> feedback.

## 0) Input Format
The user message may contain:
- TASK: exact writing prompt + required points (optional)
- STUDENT_TEXT: learner text (required)
- TEXT_TYPE: essay/email/blog (optional; default essay)
- EXPERT_ANNOTATION: structured expert markup (optional; may include errors/strengths/comments)
- CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: in-distribution labeled examples for level calibration (optional)
- RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS: similar error precedents (optional)
- RANKING_ANCHORS: reference texts with known CEFR levels for pairwise calibration (optional)

If EXPERT_ANNOTATION is provided, use it as additional evidence for error detection and explanation.
Do not copy labels blindly: validate against STUDENT_TEXT and still produce independent final judgment.
If CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES / RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS / RANKING_ANCHORS are provided, treat them as supporting context only.
Never override direct evidence from STUDENT_TEXT.

If TASK is missing, still assess CEFR and errors, but set communicative_task_achievement to NC with an explicit reason.

## 1) Level Determination
Estimate CEFR holistically and conservatively using:
- vocabulary range and precision
- grammar complexity and control
- sentence complexity (subordination, clause variety)
- cohesion and discourse organization
- register appropriacy

If a level is marked with '+' (e.g., A2+, B1+, B2+, C1+), map it to the lower canonical CEFR level (A2, B1, B2, C1).

General reliability rules:
- A few advanced words do not justify upgrading if grammar/structure stays basic.
- Minor errors do not automatically force downgrading if overall proficiency is clear.

## 2) Communicative Task Achievement (Yes / NC / No)
Return exactly one label:
- Yes: addresses prompt, covers required points, stays relevant.
- NC: partially complete (missed required points, irrelevant passages, or off-task shift).
- No: does not address task.

If TASK is missing: always NC with reason "Task not provided".

## 3) Error Markup (Strict Categories)
Use ONLY these categories:
- Lexis
- Grammar
- Punctuation
- Word order
- Sentence structure
- Text organization
- Discourse markers
- Rhetorical features
- Register
- Incomprehensibility

Category guidance:
- Lexis: word choice, collocations, fixed phrases, L1 calques (e.g., "decide a problem" -> "solve a problem", error from B1).
- Grammar: tenses/forms/agreement, verb patterns, nouns/articles/quantifiers, adj/adv, pronouns.
- Punctuation: punctuation + capitalization when relevant.
- Word order: ordering violations fixable by reordering.
- Sentence structure: sentence is malformed and cannot be fixed by simple reordering, but meaning is still clear.
- Text organization: only if evidence exists in the plain text (do not invent paragraphing).
- Discourse markers: linking words and connectors. Mark here only if misused/incorrect.
- Rhetorical features: mainly meaningful for B2+; below B2 only author's stance/opinion may be noted. Mark here only if misused/incorrect.
- Register (especially essay/formal): contractions, slang, lowercase "i", etc.
- Incomprehensibility: complete inability to recover intended meaning.

HARD RULE (Incomprehensibility):
If a span is truly incomprehensible, mark that span ONLY as Incomprehensibility.
Do not also tag lexis/grammar/word-order for that same span.
You may still note obvious spelling/punctuation separately if clear.

Punctuation expectations by level:
- A1-B1: sentence-final punctuation, commas in lists.
- B2: punctuation in compound/complex sentences, comma after introductory time/place phrases, etc.
- C1-C2: near-complete mastery including style nuances.

For each error object include:
- excerpt
- correction
- comment
- impact: High|Medium|Low
- cefr_threshold: A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2

cefr_threshold = the lowest CEFR level from which incorrect use of this element should be treated as an error in this methodology.

## 4) Strengths (Only Above Final Level)
After choosing final CEFR level, list strengths strictly ABOVE that level:
- vocabulary
- collocations
- grammar structures
- punctuation
- rhetorical/discourse features (if above level)

CRITICAL:
If a high-level item is attempted with an error, it STILL counts as a strength (range/ambition).
Add a caveat in note.

## 5) Grammar Score (0.0-1.0)
Estimate grammar_score from:
- error density and severity
- impact on meaning
- overall grammatical control

Interpretation:
- 0.90-1.00 excellent
- 0.70-0.89 good
- 0.50-0.69 acceptable
- <0.50 weak

## 6) Output JSON (ONLY JSON)
Return ONLY this structure:

{
  "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2",
  "level_basis": {
    "rule_notes": "string"
  },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes|NC|No",
  "communicative_task_notes": ["string", "string"],
  "grammar_score": 0.0,
  "corrections": [
    "excerpt -> correction (Category; threshold; impact)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "...", "correction": "...", "comment": "...", "impact": "High|Medium|Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [],
    "Punctuation": [],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "...", "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2", "category": "Vocabulary|Grammar|Punctuation|Rhetorical feature|Discourse marker", "note": "..."}
  ],
  "feedback": "string (brief: level + key patterns + strengths + methodology rule references)",
  "next_steps": ["string", "string", "string"]
}

Do not output markdown. Do not output text outside JSON.

TASK: There is an opinion that too many young people now want to get a university degree. It would be better for society if most of them start working after finishing secondary school. How much do you agree with this point of view? State your opinion and give at least two arguments to support it.
STUDENT_TEXT: With the rising popularity of people who dropped out of college and became successful, more and more young people tend to end their education process as soon as they graduate from school or even earlier. However, there are people who think that the trend to get higher education among younger generation is toxic and meaningless, because if you find a job early, you will be successful in life. 

Personally, I strongly disagree with that point of view. First, when a person graduates from school, he/she has extremely limited knowledge in almost every sphere and no experience in adult life. Getting an university degree can broaden your vision on a particular sphere of interest, help you gain knowledge and skills much more quickier than you'd have gained 'em while working in whatever company. Additionally, while a person studies at university, he/she learns how to live on their owns, separated from their parents, and the adaptation period goes much more easier than if a person decided to find a job right after graduating out of school. 

Also, university is a great place to skyrocket your social skills, soft-skills and common sense. That happens because you interact with people of your age with similar goals, hobbies, manner of talking, and that makes the process of the personality development smooth like butter. On the other hand, when you start working somewhere, there will be a little chance that you'll meet people your age or people you simply would like to interact with. In the end, we are still children at 18 y.o. 

In conclusion, I want to say that every single person should decide for themselves whether they need degree or not; whether they need to find a job after school or not. After all, every individual has their own perception on any decision in their life. 
TEXT_TYPE: essay

What is your opinion?","student_text":"The author claims that success is explained only by a hard work. I am absolutely agree with this statement. In our big world each person has some talent. There are many people with the same talents and only the hard work can allow to win in this competition of talents. First and foremost, there are a cases when the person have some talent but not reach a success. My brother was a scientist and he had dreamed about studing in the prestigious foreign university. He was not silly çat all. His inteligence quality score is one hundred and fourty two. But he was lazy and he had not practiced with math. Thus, he had not passed math exam in any foreign university, but it was possible. Last but not the least, some people want to realise their dream so much that any negative circumstances cannot stop it. When my mother was a kid she wanted about be a doctor. Her dream was to be a surgeon, bur she had the disease with hand which made impossible her plans about profession. Surpisingly, now she is a surgeon. State your opinion and give at least two arguments to support it.","student_text":"There is an opinion that young people should go to work after school. I cannnot agree with this point. Let me explain.

Of course, going to work early sounds good. A person begins to gain work experience, builds a career and earns money for himself. This is a good opportunity for people who are confident in themselves and have decided on the field in which they want to work.

But there is different situation. School graduates often don't know what they want to do and where they want to work. Or maybe they want to work in science or in other difficult area. In this situation university is the best choice. In the university people study different complex subjects. They can try various fields and choose what they like. And knowledge that university gives is complex.

To sum it up, there is no one right answer to this question.The choice depends on the situation and needs of a particular person.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":6,"errors":[{"text":"cannnot ","labels":["Spelling"],"start":72,"end":80},{"text":"different situation","labels":["Grammar"],"start":392,"end":411},{"text":" other","labels":["Grammar"],"start":540,"end":546},{"text":"In the university","labels":["Grammar"],"start":612,"end":629},{"text":"knowledge","labels":["Grammar"],"start":730,"end":739},{"text":" university","labels":["Grammar"],"start":744,"end":755},{"text":"To sum it up,","labels":["Discourse markers"],"start":775,"end":788},{"text":"there is no one right answer","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":789,"end":817},{"text":"needs","labels":["Grammar"],"start":875,"end":880}],"strengths":[{"text":"Of course,","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":120,"end":130},{"text":"But there is different situation.","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":379,"end":412},{"text":"Or maybe they want","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":497,"end":515},{"text":"They can try","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":671,"end":683}],"comments":[]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07013","cefr_level":"A2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hi, im styduing in the MIPT - in one of the hardest technical universitiyes in Russia, and i want to give you some advices.

My first advidce - it to sleep from 7 to 9 hours (how much exactly depends from you) in day, and you should stand from up and go to bed in one time in day. With sleeping regulary in one time you will feel better and maybe you will feel better.

Next advice - it to make calendar with notions, or something like this, it helps to plan day wery succesfull. Sometimes procrastination wins you, but with notions it weel be easier to make hometasks in time.

The third advice - food delivery, if you have not enought time to cook something, or to go somewhere in big sity you can easily get food from delivery services. But sometimes people eats only food from delivery, and even don't go out from their rooms, of course it isn't good.

And the last what i want to say - probably your biggest troubles with time it that you have not hard character. Without this super ability it hard to self-controle, may be you should to ask your friends wathching what are you doing in free time, they can anounce if you spend time on games or other time-killers.
